title: Dry-Salt-Cured American Ackee Hash
description: A bitter, crumbly, and quick-fix American dish featuring ackee, salt-baked with a dash of flavor.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor or a clay pot to 375°F (190°C).
In a small bowl, mix together salt and black pepper.
Add the chopped ackee, red bell pepper, onion, and garlic to the bowl and toss to coat with the salt mixture.
Transfer the mixture to the preheated tandoor or clay pot and bake for 20 minutes.
After 20 minutes, remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Add the olive oil and butter to the mixture and stir to combine.
Return the pot to the heat and continue baking for another 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat and let the mixture cool completely.
Use a fork to break up the mixture and serve hot.
